assertions.description: CHECK_NEEDS_FIX: The UEXT1 (BH10S, 10-pin connector) routes UEXT_SCL/UEXT_SDA (I2C with 2.2k pull-ups to +3.3V), UEXT_SCK/UEXT_MOSI/UEXT_MISO/UEXT_#SS (SPI full-duplex), and UEXT_TxD/UEXT_RxD (UART) through the 74ALVC164245DL bidirectional voltage translator (U3). The analyzer correctly identifies all three buses with appropriate pull-ups and signal groupings.

CHECK_NEEDS_FIX: The analyzer correctly identifies VR1 (MCP1825-ADJE/DC) as an LDO regulator and detects the 15.92 Hz RC low-pass filter formed by R7 (100k/1%) and C5 (100nF) on the Vdac output. This filter smooths the I2C DAC output before feeding into the voltage regulation feedback network. The RC filter detection is technically correct; input and output nets are unnamed because the Vdac net is involved in the pin mapping error described separately.

INCORRECT: The MCP47A1T-A0E DAC (U1) has pin 3 named SCL (I2C clock input) and pin 5 named VOUT (DAC analog output). The net file confirms pin 3 connects to UEXT_SCL and pin 5 connects to Vdac. However the legacy .sch parser reports the 'Vdac' net as containing U1 pin 3 (SCL). This causes a spurious third I2C entry: 'Vdac' is listed as an I2C SCL line for U1 in bus_analysis.i2c[2], when Vdac is actually the DAC analog output. The true SCL connection (U1 pin 3 on UEXT_SCL) is already correctly captured in bus_analysis.i2c[0]. This is a pin-to-net mapping error in the KiCad 5 legacy schematic parser.
